bullet_points: Load all six stacked signal maps and all noise maps (SO LAT for 90/150/217 GHz and Planck FFP10 for 353/545/857 GHz) using the specified loading pattern.
Print per-channel variance statistics to confirm the 10¹⁵ dynamic range disparity.
Compute and save the tSZ normalization parameters (mean and std over all 1523 patches) to a file.
Produce a diagnostic plot showing per-channel signal variance and the tSZ histogram before and after standardization, saving it to the database.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Preprocessing, Normalization, and Diagnostic Analysis

bullet_points: Compute auto- and cross-power spectra for all 6 frequency channels and the tSZ ground truth on noise-injected observed maps using utils.powers with window_alpha=0.5.
Estimate noise power spectra separately from dedicated noise-only realizations and add them to the signal covariance.
Construct the (N_freq+1) x (N_freq+1) x N_ell covariance cube and the tSZ cross-vector.
Apply Tikhonov regularization, invert the matrices per ell-bin, and save the resulting per-ell Wiener filter weights to disk.
Produce a diagnostic plot of the diagonal auto-spectra and selected off-diagonal cross-spectra.
Identify high-mass halos by thresholding tsz.npy at peak y > 5e-5.
Stack 30x30 pixel cutouts centered on peaks to compute the radially averaged profile.
Convolve this profile with the appropriate Gaussian beam for each of the six frequency channels.
Save the beam-convolved profile templates to disk and produce a plot for visual comparison.
Apply the MWF operator to the multi-frequency observation vector to produce the reconstructed Compton-y map.
Implement an ILC baseline as a uniform-weight linear combination of the six frequency channels after spectral response normalization.
Save both MWF and ILC reconstructions to disk.
Produce plots of the transfer function and cross-correlation coefficient for both methods on the same axes.
Apply the matched filter (using the beam-convolved profile from Step 3) to the reconstructed y-map in Fourier space.
Normalize the filtered map to SNR units using the local noise estimate.
Threshold at SNR > 4 and SNR > 5 to identify candidate clusters.
Construct a ground-truth catalog from noiseless tsz.npy maps using the same peak-finding procedure with a minimum separation criterion.
Calculate completeness and purity vs. peak Compton-y and local CIB intensity at 857 GHz.
Generate scatter plots of recovered vs. true integrated-Y and mass bias vs. true peak Compton-y.
Print all numerical results (bin edges, completeness, purity, mean bias, scatter) to the console and save to a structured file.
Report all key numerical results quantitatively, including the ell range where the transfer function exceeds 0.5, 50%/90% completeness thresholds, and purity at those thresholds.
Compare MWF versus ILC performance on all metrics.
Discuss the physical interpretation of CIB contamination and the limitations of the Wiener filter at high ell.
Ensure all intermediate outputs are referenced and interpreted correctly.
